Each year volunteers from England work abroad: teachers, nurses, engineers, and people with special skills. They do not go for money. Volunteers from Voluntary Service Overseas (VSO) receive the (17)__________ pay as a local colleague.
VSO first (18) __________ in 1958 when 14 young school leavers went away for a year.

Only three years later there were 176 volunteers in 25 different countries, and ten years later there were 15,000 volunteers. At first, almost everyone (19)__________young, and without very much experience.
Now more than 20,000 people from England have worked abroad as volunteers, some are young, (20)__________are old.
Abroad they live simply with the local people, and they return (21)__________ England with great understanding of other people in other parts of the world.
